title: RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging
learningObjectives: Compare **Kafka**, **RabbitMQ**, **SQS**, and **SNS** on semantics, replay, and ops
Operate **SQS** with **visibility timeouts**, **long polling**, and **DLQ** strategies
Tune **RabbitMQ** **prefetch** and **dead-letter** routing for reliable task queues
Choose **FIFO** vs **standard** queues with clear throughput and ordering trade-offs
Design **cross-system** bridges without losing ordering or amplifying duplicates

sections.title: Why RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging matters
sections.content:
Not every problem is **Kafka-shaped**. Interviewers reward knowing when **SQS**, **SNS**, **Rabbit**, and **Kafka** each win, especially around **replay**, **routing**, and **ops** **surface** area.

This day also ties **cloud** economics: **polling**, **visibility**, and **fan-out** patterns affect bills and **reliability**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging
sections.content:
### Day 66 — **RabbitMQ** + **AWS** **messaging**

### 1. RabbitMQ
**Exchanges**, **bindings**, **queues**, **prefetch**, **DLX**.

### 2. SQS / SNS
**Standard** vs **FIFO**, **visibility**, **long** polling, **DLQ**.

### 3. Event abstraction
**EventBridge** **rules** and **schemas**.

### 4. Kafka contrast
**Replay** **log** vs **work** **queue**.

### 5. Failure modes
**Visibility** **too** **short**, **retry** storms, **hot** **queues**.

### 6. Story
**SQS** **visibility** too low → duplicate **payments**; extend **timeout** + **idempotent** workers.

sections.title: Basic — SQS vs Kafka headline
sections.output:
SQS: managed queue with visibility timeout and DLQ
SNS: pub/sub fan-out bus
Kafka: durable log with replay and consumer groups

sections.title: Intermediate — Assignment one-liner
sections.description: Print SQS vs Kafka selection line.
sections.output: SQS: managed queue/task; Kafka: log streaming/replay

sections.title: Advanced — Visibility math toy
sections.description: If handler 25s, pick visibility at least 30.

sections.title: SNS fan-out to multiple SQS queues
sections.description: Fan-out pattern with independent consumer scaling.

sections.title: Exercise — RabbitMQ & AWS messaging — selection (Day 66 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly**: SQS: managed queue/task; Kafka: log streaming/replay
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging
sections.content:
## Use cases — **RabbitMQ & AWS messaging**

### 1) **Job** **queue** with **SQS** **standard** **queue**
**Context:** **Worker** **fleet** **pulls** **tasks**; **bursty** **load**.
**Why Day 66:** **Visibility** **timeout** vs **processing** **p99**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Set **VisibilityTimeout** > **p99** **handler**; **heartbeat** **extend** for **long** **jobs**.
2. **Dead-letter** **queue** + **redrive** **policy** for **inspection**.
3. **Idempotent** **workers** for **at-least-once** **delivery**.
**Failure hook:** **Duplicate** **work** when **visibility** **expires** **early**.

### 2) **SNS** **fan-out** to **Lambda** + **SQS**
**Context:** **Domain** **events** trigger **multiple** **subscribers**.
**Why Day 66:** **Fan-out** **latency** and **failure** **isolation** per **subscriber**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Per** **subscriber** **queue** so **one** **bad** **consumer** **does** **not** **block** **others**.
2. **Filter** **policies** on **SNS** to **cut** **cost** and **noise**.
**Failure hook:** **Lambda** **throttle** → **backlog** on **one** **queue** only.

### 3) **RabbitMQ** **for** **low-latency** **commands**
**Context:** **Internal** **tooling** needs **push** **semantics** and **per-message** **ack**.
**Why Day 66:** **Prefetch** and **DLX** **patterns** differ from **Kafka** **logs**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Tune **prefetch** for **fair** **work** **distribution**.
2. **Dead-letter** **exchange** + **TTL** **for** **timeouts**.
3. Do **not** **assume** **replay** **history** like **Kafka** **retention**.
**Failure hook:** **Memory** **pressure** when **consumers** **stall** with **high** **prefetch**.

### 4) **Hybrid** **Kafka** + **SQS** **edge**
**Context:** **Kafka** **inside** **VPC**; **SQS** at **edge** for **partner** **callbacks**.
**Why Day 66:** **Contract** and **ordering** **differ**; **translate** **explicitly**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Bridge** **service** **maps** **keys** to **SQS** **message** **groups** (**FIFO**) if **needed**.
2. Document **ordering** **guarantees** **per** **integration**.
**Failure hook:** **Partners** **assume** **Kafka**-style **replay** on **SQS**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: When **RabbitMQ** fits?
conceptual.answer: **AMQP** broker with **flexible** routing (**exchanges**, **queues**), good for **task** workloads and **complex** topologies on **self-managed** or **hosted** Rabbit. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **When **RabbitMQ** fits?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Kafka** vs **queue** mental model?
conceptual.answer: **Kafka**: **durable** **log** with **replay** and **consumer groups**; **classic** **queues**: **delete** or **ack** semantics after consumption. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****Kafka** vs **queue** mental model?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **SQS**?
conceptual.answer: Managed **queue** with **visibility timeout**, **long polling**, **DLQ** integration—**no** log replay like Kafka. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **SQS**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **SNS**?
conceptual.answer: Pub/sub **fan-out** to many subscribers including SQS, Lambda, HTTP. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **SNS**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **SQS** **FIFO** vs **standard**?
conceptual.answer: FIFO gives **ordering** + **dedupe** with lower **throughput**; standard is **at-least-once** high throughput. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****SQS** **FIFO** vs **standard**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: **Visibility timeout** pitfall?
conceptual.answer: Processing longer than timeout causes **duplicate** delivery to another consumer. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

conceptual.answer: Limits **unacked** messages per consumer balancing **fairness** and **memory**. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. conceptual.answer: Route **rejected**/**expired** messages for inspection. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. conceptual.answer: EventBridge offers **schema** registry, **content** filtering, **SaaS** integrations—different **routing** ergonomics. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. conceptual.answer: Both **streaming** logs; operational model and ecosystem differ—pick with **cloud** constraints. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: What is the risk of **unclean leader election** on a financial topic?
conceptual.answer: Promoting a **non-ISR** replica can **truncate** the log and **lose** acknowledged data from the prior leader’s perspective—**catastrophic** for **ledger** topics. Teams disable it where **durability** beats **availability**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

conceptual.question: How does **Kafka Connect** **task restart** differ from a **consumer** restart?
conceptual.answer: Connect tasks have **worker** coordination, **offset** storage in **internal** topics, and **connector**-specific failure modes—**serialization** errors, **DLQs**, **rebalance** across workers. Treat it as **another** distributed client with its own dashboards.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// WaitTimeSeconds=20 long poll** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: In Streams, what does **`commitIntervalMs`** interact with?
codeBased.answer: It controls how often **processed** state **commits** and interacts with **latency** vs **durability** of **state** and **punctuators**. **Changelog** replay still backs recovery, but **tuning** impacts failure windows.

codeBased.question: What AWS **SQS** **`VisibilityTimeout`** protects against?
codeBased.answer: It hides a message from other consumers while one **worker** processes it. Too short → **duplicate** processing; too long → **slow** recovery on crashes.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Lambda** **throttles** on **SQS** burst.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Increase concurrency limits.

**(2) Root cause** Downstream saturation.

**(3) Fix** Kinesis or batching tuning.

**(4) Prevention** AWS support ticket. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Lambda** **throttles** on **SQS** burst.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Bug:** **Visibility** too low vs **P99** handler.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Duplicates spike.

**(2) Root cause** Timeout mismatch.

**(3) Fix** Raise visibility + heartbeat pattern.

**(4) Prevention** Metrics. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.answer: For ****Bug:** **Visibility** too low vs **P99** handler.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Design:** Need **replay** month of audit.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** SQS inadequate alone.

**(2) Root cause** No log retention.

**(3) Fix** Kafka or S3 event archive.

**(4) Prevention** ADR. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Design:** Need **replay** month of audit.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** Rabbit **memory** alarm.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Prefetch + backlog.

**(2) Root cause** Consumers slower than producers.

**(3) Fix** Scale consumers + quarantine overloaded queues.

**(4) Prevention** Runbook. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Ops:** Rabbit **memory** alarm.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Cost:** **SNS** **HTTP** endpoints flaky.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Retry storms.

**(2) Root cause** No backoff.

**(3) Fix** Exponential backoff + DLQ subscriptions.

**(4) Prevention** FinOps. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Silent drops.

**(2) Root cause** Mapping error.

**(3) Fix** Parallel run with shadow traffic.

**(4) Prevention** Verification job. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

wrongAnswers: **Kafka** can always emulate **Rabbit** **routing** trivially — **Correction:** Different **primitives**; often need careful design.
**Visibility** timeout solves poison messages — **Correction:** Need **DLQ** and redrive policies.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which statement best describes **SQS visibility timeout** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **SQS visibility timeout** belongs to the operational model for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **SNS fan-out**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **SNS fan-out** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **FIFO queue**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **FIFO queue** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.explanation: **deduplication ID** belongs to the operational model for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] You see misconfiguration around **RabbitMQ exchange** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **RabbitMQ exchange** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which statement best describes **SNS fan-out** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **SNS fan-out** belongs to the operational model for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **FIFO queue**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **FIFO queue** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **deduplication ID**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **deduplication ID** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which statement best describes **RabbitMQ exchange** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **RabbitMQ exchange** belongs to the operational model for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] You see misconfiguration around **routing key** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **routing key** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **dead letter exchange**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **dead letter exchange** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **prefetch** belongs to the operational model for **RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] You see misconfiguration around **competing consumers** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **competing consumers** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **message TTL**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **message TTL** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

sections.title: RabbitMQ & AWS Messaging — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tech | Recall | Recall |
| SQS | Visibility timeout, DLQ | Visibility timeout, DLQ |
| SNS | Pub/sub fan-out | Pub/sub fan-out |
| FIFO | Order + dedupe limits | Order + dedupe limits |
| EventBridge | Rules + schemas | Rules + schemas |
| Rabbit | Exchanges + bindings | Exchanges + bindings |
| prefetch | Consumer fairness | Consumer fairness |
| DLX | Rabbit dead letters | Rabbit dead letters |
| long poll | SQS efficiency | SQS efficiency |
| Kinesis | Streaming alternative | Streaming alternative |
| Kafka | Replay log | Replay log |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
